title: Jagannath Temple, Angul
tagline: Jagannath Temple, Angul — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Saila Srikhetra is a local Hindu temple site located in the district of Angul in the state of Odisha.
overview.description: Saila Srikhetra is a local Hindu temple site located in the district of Angul in the state of Odisha. It is a regional spiritual center where people worship Jagannath, similar to how people worship at the Jagannath Temple in Puri.

topPlaces.name: Black Diamond Museum, Angul
topPlaces.description: Black Diamond Museum and Library (Odia: ବ୍ଲାକ୍ ଡାୟମଣ୍ଡ ମ୍ୟୁଜିୟମ୍ ଏବଂ ଲାଇବ୍ରେରି; Oriya: Blāk ḍāyamaṇḍ myujiẏam ebaṁ lāibreri, pronounced [Blāk ḍāyamaṇḍ myujiẏam ebaṁ lāibreri] ), popularly known as Black Diamond Museum or Black Diamond Complex, is a digital combined cultural museum and library located in Angul, Odisha. Established in 2024, it focuses on preservation of the history, technology, and heritage of coal mining in the Talcher coalfields and the Angul region.

topPlaces.name: Nalconagar, Angul
topPlaces.description: Nalconagar is a census town in Angul district in the Indian state of Odisha. Nalconagar, Angul is a suburb of Angul, Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Angul Aluminum Park
topPlaces.description: Angul Aluminium Park (also referred to as Angul Aluminium Park Private Limited) is an industrial park located in Angul, in the state of Odisha. It is developed by National Aluminium Company Limited and the Odisha Industrial Infrastructure Development Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Angul Stadium
topPlaces.description: Angul Stadium is a cricket ground in Angul, Odisha. The ground was established in 1999 and is owned by Angul District Sports Association.

topPlaces.name: Electric Loco Shed, Angul
topPlaces.description: Electric Loco Shed, Angul is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Angul of the East Coast Railway zone in Odisha, India. As of 1 June 2026, there are 301 locomotives in the shed.

topPlaces.name: Angul
topPlaces.description: Angul (also known as Anugul) is a city, municipality and the headquarters of Angul district in the state of Odisha, India. Angul has an average elevation of 195 m (640 ft) above sea level and total area of 6232 km2.

topPlaces.name: Malisahi
topPlaces.description: Malisahi is a village in Angul district, Odisha, India. It is situated in the side of long-standing river.

topPlaces.name: Talcher Thermal Power Station
topPlaces.description: Talcher Thermal Power station is located in Talcher sub-division of Angul district in the Indian state of Odisha Pin.759101. The existing plant was closed on 31 March 2021 with the plans to set up a new plant still in discussion phase.

items.note: Black Diamond Museum and Library (Odia: ବ୍ଲାକ୍ ଡାୟମଣ୍ଡ ମ୍ୟୁଜିୟମ୍ ଏବଂ ଲାଇବ୍ରେରି; Oriya: Blāk ḍāyamaṇḍ myujiẏam ebaṁ lāibreri, pronounced [Blāk ḍāyamaṇḍ myujiẏam ebaṁ lāibreri] ), popularly known as Black Diamond Museum or Black Diamond Complex, is a digital combined cultural museum and library located in Angul, Odisha. Established in 2024, it focuses on preservation of the history, technology, and heritage of coal mining in the Talcher coalfields and the Angul region.
Nalconagar is a census town in Angul district in the Indian state of Odisha. Nalconagar, Angul is a suburb of Angul, Odisha.

Angul Aluminium Park (also referred to as Angul Aluminium Park Private Limited) is an industrial park located in Angul, in the state of Odisha. It is developed by National Aluminium Company Limited and the Odisha Industrial Infrastructure Development Corporation.
Angul Stadium is a cricket ground in Angul, Odisha. The ground was established in 1999 and is owned by Angul District Sports Association.

Electric Loco Shed, Angul is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Angul of the East Coast Railway zone in Odisha, India. As of 1 June 2026, there are 301 locomotives in the shed.

Angul (also known as Anugul) is a city, municipality and the headquarters of Angul district in the state of Odisha, India. Angul has an average elevation of 195 m (640 ft) above sea level and total area of 6232 km2.
Malisahi is a village in Angul district, Odisha, India. It is situated in the side of long-standing river.

Talcher Thermal Power station is located in Talcher sub-division of Angul district in the Indian state of Odisha Pin.759101. The existing plant was closed on 31 March 2021 with the plans to set up a new plant still in discussion phase.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jagannath Temple, Angul?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jagannath Temple, Angul is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jagannath Temple, Angul?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (96 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (96 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jagannath Temple, Angul?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jagannath Temple, Angul at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jagannath Temple, Angul?
faq.a: Stays in Jagannath Temple, Angul start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jagannath Temple, Angul?
faq.a: The top attractions are Black Diamond Museum, Angul, Nalconagar, Angul, Angul Aluminum Park, Angul Stadium, Electric Loco Shed, Angul.
